steps.title: Identify what the pronoun refers to
steps.content: The sentence says astronomers "attributed ______ to the gravitational influence of the previously unknown planet." The pronoun stands in for what was attributed — the wobble that the astronomers were observing. "The wobble" is singular and non-human.

steps.title: Choose the pronoun that matches
steps.content: Rule: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number. "The wobble" is singular. A. "them" — plural personal pronoun. Fails: "the wobble" is singular, not plural. B. "it" — singular personal pronoun for non-human nouns. Correctly replaces "the wobble." Correct. C. "these" — plural demonstrative pronoun. Fails: number mismatch; also implies pointing to multiple things just mentioned, not one wobble. D. "each" — distributive pronoun used when referring to individual members of a group separately. Fails: there is no group of wobbles to distribute across; the referent is a single phenomenon. That matches choice B — B is correct.
